descriptions.golden: Automatically clicks Golden Cookies and Reindeer as soon as they appear. Reindeer only appear during seasonal events.
descriptions.wrath: Automatically clicks Wrath Cookies (red). May trigger negative grandmapocalypse effects.
descriptions.bigCookie: Continuously clicks the big cookie as fast as possible to accumulate cookies.
descriptions.wrinklers: Pops all Wrinklers once they have all reached their final stage, collecting their cookies.
descriptions.spell: Safe-casts Force the Hand of Fate when the Grimoire is full. It predicts the outcome from the game's seed and only casts when a golden cookie is guaranteed, skipping predicted backfires (wrath cookies). On a predicted backfire it instead casts one cheap Conjure Baked Goods to bank a few cookies and reshuffle the draw.
descriptions.fortune: Clicks fortune messages in the news ticker to collect their reward.
descriptions.dragon: Automatically levels up the Krumblor dragon when resources allow.
descriptions.dragonAura: Sets the dragon's primary aura to Radiant Appetite (x2 cookie production) once it becomes selectable at dragon level 19. Switching an aura sacrifices your highest building, exactly as the in-game menu does, so this happens only once. Opt-in.
descriptions.market: Trades the Bank's stock market for you: buys a stock below its mean price while it trends up, sells the whole stock on a downturn once it beats your buy-in, and hires brokers to cut trading fees. Buildings and upgrades keep priority, so it only invests the surplus above the Lucky Reserve; selling is always allowed and profits count toward cookies earned. Investing carries risks: past cookies are no guarantee of future cookies. Trade responsibly.
descriptions.office: Upgrades the Bank's offices for more stock storage, so the market can hold bigger positions and bank bigger trades. Each upgrade sacrifices cursors and requires the Cursor building to reach a sugar-lump level, so it only ever triggers once you have levelled your Cursors. Opt-in.
descriptions.garden: Harvests mature garden plants just before they wither and replants the same species, for passive farm income. Immortal plants are left untouched. Warning: this destroys whatever is growing, so do not use it while breeding mutations.
descriptions.cloverGarden: Fills the whole garden with Golden clover and keeps it maintained, for a large permanent boost to golden cookie frequency (+3% per mature tile). Requires the Turbo-charged soil upgrade (free planting) and the Golden clover seed unlocked — the toggle stays hidden otherwise, and it never spends cookies. Use Clay soil for +25% effect. WARNING: it harvests and replaces your other (non-immortal) plants to convert the plot — don't use it on a garden you're cross-breeding.
descriptions.breeder: Auto-breeds the cross-breeding chain to unlock the Golden clover seed (Baker's wheat → Thumbcorn → Cronerice → Gildmillet → Golden clover). It keeps the plot in a breeding layout and harvests each new mutation once it matures. Requires the Turbo-charged soil upgrade (free planting); it never spends cookies and hides once Golden clover is unlocked. Use Woodchips soil for triple mutation speed. WARNING: it takes over and replants the whole garden; don't use it on plants you want to keep.
descriptions.pantheon: Slots a set-and-forget idle line-up in the Temple's pantheon. It spends real worship swaps (which recharge slowly), one per tick, then leaves the pantheon alone.
descriptions.oneMind: Allows Auto Buy to purchase the 'One Mind' upgrade (Elder Covenant path). Use with caution.
descriptions.elderPact: Auto-buys Elder Pact to take the Grandmapocalypse to stage 3 (maximum wrinkler spawns and wrath cookie odds, no production downside). Off by default; appears only while One Mind is on. Note: Elder Pact is also the Portal building synergy condition, so this stays a deliberate opt-in.
descriptions.lumps: Harvests a sugar lump automatically once it is ripe, for a guaranteed gain.
descriptions.luckyReserve: Keeps a cookie reserve (about 6000x your CPS) so Golden 'Lucky!' cookies pay their maximum — a deliberate savings mode. While your bank is below the reserve, the mod stops buying (buildings, upgrades, stocks) until it is reached, then spends only the surplus above it. Best turned on to save up for a Lucky, not during a growth phase.
descriptions.dragonSacrifice: Lets the dragon auto-level sacrifice 100 buildings per level to fully train Krumblor. Off by default; appears only while Dragon is on. Warning: Auto Buy will immediately rebuy the sacrificed buildings. The last two levels are the costliest: they sacrifice 50 then 200 of every building, so keep enough cookies to rebuild before they fire.
descriptions.buyBuildings: Automatically targets the single most profitable building: its CPS/price ratio with synergies, plus how close it is to its next tier milestone (which unlocks a ×2 upgrade). It saves up for that best building instead of spending on lesser ones, so it may pause while cookies accumulate. If Auto Buy Upgrades is also on, it waits while an affordable upgrade is still in the store.
descriptions.buyUpgrades: Automatically buys every affordable store upgrade, except the irreversible Grandmapocalypse ones. One Mind is bought only if its own toggle is on.
descriptions.strategy: Preset selector that configures the strategy toggles in one move. Grind: pure CPS growth — buys buildings and upgrades, no Stock Market or reserve. Investor: max income — Lucky Reserve, aggressive Stock Market, offices, brokers and Pantheon. Manual: independent toggles, changes nothing. Editing a toggle by hand switches the selector back to Manual.
